mcqs.question: Which color space is most suitable for applying smoothing only to the luminance component while preserving color information?
mcqs.explanation: HSI color space separates intensity (I) from color information (Hue and Saturation), making it ideal for luminance-only processing while preserving color integrity.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of component-wise color smoothing in RGB space compared to vector-based approaches?

options.text: Introduction of color artifacts and bleeding

mcqs.explanation: Component-wise processing in RGB can introduce color artifacts and bleeding because it processes each channel independently without considering color vector relationships.

mcqs.question: In vector median filtering for color smoothing, how is the output pixel value determined?

options.text: Average of all color vectors in the neighborhood
Median of each color channel computed separately
Color vector that minimizes the sum of distances to other vectors
Color vector with the maximum frequency in the neighborhood

mcqs.explanation: Vector median filtering selects the color vector that minimizes the sum of distances to all other vectors in the neighborhood, preserving edge information and reducing impulse noise.

mcqs.question: Which of the following techniques would be most effective for reducing salt-and-pepper noise in a color image while preserving edges?

options.text: Component-wise Gaussian filtering in RGB space

mcqs.explanation: Vector median filtering is particularly effective for impulse noise (salt-and-pepper) because it selects the most representative color vector while preserving edges, unlike linear filters that blur edges.

mcqs.question: When applying unsharp masking to color images, what precaution is necessary to avoid invalid color values?

options.text: Clamp the resulting values to the valid range

mcqs.explanation: Unsharp masking can produce values outside the valid color range (e.g., below 0 or above 255 for 8-bit images), so clamping is necessary to prevent invalid color values.
